marcus

- the retention problem is huge. artists jump between redbubble society6 and etsy bc none of them are good enough on their own
- if we can be the one platform that does everything thats the whole play

send me the notion link ill dig in this week. i can start on the marketing side and competitor pricing stuff

also we keep this between us for now ya? no need to tell anyone til we have something real

trev

From: Vermont Health & Wellness Newsletter <newsletter@vthealthwellness.com>
Date: April 23, 2026
Subject: 7 Tips for Surviving Spring Allergy Season

Spring is here, and so is pollen season! Here are 7 tips to help you breathe easier:

1. Check the daily pollen count before heading outside
2. Keep windows closed during high pollen days
3. Shower and change clothes after spending time outdoors
4. Start taking antihistamines before symptoms peak
5. Use a saline nasal rinse daily
6. Wear sunglasses to protect your eyes
7. Consider a HEPA air purifier for your bedroom

Vermont's tree pollen season typically peaks in mid-April through May. Birch, oak, and maple are the biggest culprits in our region.

Stay healthy!
Vermont Health & Wellness

emails.subject: Weekly Fishing Report - Lake Champlain, May 11-17
emails.content:
Weekly Fishing Report - May 11-17, 2026

Lake Champlain

Largemouth Bass: Good. Shallow bays warming up, topwater producing in early morning. Focus on rocky points and weed edges.

Smallmouth Bass: Fair to Good. Moving into pre-spawn staging areas. Drop-shot and ned rigs working well in 8-15 feet.

Northern Pike: Good. Active in weedy shallows. Spinnerbaits and large streamers producing.

Walleye: Fair. Still deep in main lake. Trolling crankbaits 15-25 feet.

Lake Trout: Slow. Starting to move deeper as surface temps rise.

Water temp: 54-58F (main lake), 60-64F (shallow bays)

For full reports and regulations, visit vtfishandwildlife.com/fishing

Vermont Fish & Wildlife Department
